name: Turkey (Türkiye)
overview: Turkey's Law on the Protection of Personal Data No. 6698 (KVKK, 2016) was modelled on the pre-GDPR EU Directive. Major 2024 amendments aligned key areas with the GDPR: new lawful bases for sensitive data, and a reformed cross-border transfer regime (adequacy decisions, standard contracts filed with the Authority, BCRs). The Personal Data Protection Authority (KVKK) enforces actively, including against foreign companies.

legal_instruments.title: Law on the Protection of Personal Data No. 6698
legal_instruments.summary: Comprehensive law: processing conditions, sensitive-data rules, VERBIS controller registry, breach notification (72 hours per Board decision), and administrative fines. Amended March 2024 to modernise sensitive-data processing and cross-border transfers.

legal_instruments.title: Regulation on the Procedures and Principles Regarding the Transfer of Personal Data Abroad
legal_instruments.summary: Implements the amended Art. 9 transfer regime: adequacy decisions, appropriate safeguards including standard contracts (to be filed with KVKK within 5 business days) and binding corporate rules, plus incidental-transfer derogations.

developments.title: Grace period for legacy transfer mechanism ends
developments.summary: The explicit-consent basis for routine cross-border transfers expired, requiring controllers to move to standard contracts, BCRs or other Art. 9 mechanisms.

developments.title: KVKK amendments enacted in judicial reform package
developments.summary: Law No. 7499 amended KVKK Arts. 6 and 9, adding GDPR-style conditions for sensitive data and a tiered cross-border transfer regime effective 1 June 2024.

penalties: Administrative fines (2025 revalued amounts) up to roughly TRY 13-15 million for the most serious violations (e.g. VERBIS and data-security breaches), adjusted annually; criminal sanctions under the Penal Code for unlawful recording/disclosure.
cross_border: Post-2024: adequacy decisions by the Board; absent adequacy, appropriate safeguards (standard contract filed with KVKK, BCRs, written undertakings with Board approval); limited derogations for incidental transfers.
